volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Philoctetes (Section 1030 - Section 1205)

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: ὃς οὐδέν εἰμι καὶ τέθνηχʼ ὑμῖν πάλαι .
sections.translation: I am nothing , and , as far as you were concerned , I have long been dead . You creature abhorred by the gods , how is it that you no longer find me crippled and foul-smelling ? How , if I sail with you , can you burn sacrifices to the gods , or make libations any more ? That was your pretext for casting me away in the first place .

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.translation: A cruel death for you !— and die you shall for your unjust treatment of me , if the gods care for justice . But I know that they do care for it , since you would never have made this voyage for one so miserable , unless some god-sent goad had driven you after me .

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ πατρῴα γῆ θεοί τʼ ἐπόψιοι ,
sections.translation: O , fatherland , and you watchful gods , bring your vengeance , bring your vengeance on them all after so long , if at all you pity me ! Yes , my life merits pity . Yet if I were to see those men overthrown , I would believe that I had escaped my plague .

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: βαρύς τε καὶ βαρεῖαν ὁ ξένος φάτιν
sections.translation: Bitter is the stranger , and bitter his words , Odysseus . They do not bend before the storm of his troubles .

sections.label: Section 1047
sections.original: πόλλʼ ἂν λέγειν ἔχοιμι πρὸς τὰ τοῦδʼ ἔπη ,
sections.translation: I could say much in answer to his claims , if time allowed ; but now I can say one thing only . What kind of man the occasion demands , that kind of man am I .

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.translation: And accordingly , where the judgment at hand is of just and good men , you could find no man more pious than me . Victory , however , is my inborn desire in every field — save with regard to you . To you , in this case , I will gladly give way . Yes , release him , and lay not another finger upon him .

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.translation: Let him stay here . We have no further need of you , now that we have these weapons . For Teucer is there among our forces , well-skilled in this craft , as am I , and I believe that I can master this bow in no way worse than you , and point it with no worse a hand .

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: τί δῆτα σοῦ δεῖ ; χαῖρε τὴν Λῆμνον πατῶν ·
sections.translation: So what need is there of you ? Farewell ! Enjoy your strolls on Lemnos ! We must be going . And perhaps your onetime prize will bring me the honor which ought to have been your own .

sections.label: Section 1063
sections.original: οἴμοι · τί δράσω δύσμορος ; σὺ τοῖς ἐμοῖς
sections.translation: Ah , no , what shall I do ? Are you to shine before the Argives graced with my arms ?

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: μή μʼ ἀντιφώνει μηδέν , ὡς στείχοντα δή .
sections.translation: Argue with me no more . I am going .

sections.label: Section 1066
sections.original: ὦ σπέρμʼ Ἀχιλλέως , οὐδὲ σοῦ φωνῆς ἔτι
sections.translation: Seed of Achilles , will you , too , grant me your voice no more , but leave without a word ?

sections.label: Section 1068
sections.original: χώρει σύ · μὴ πρόσλευσσε , γενναῖός περ ὤν ,
sections.translation: Come on ! Do not look at him , kind and noble though you are . Do not obliterate our good fortune .

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: ἦ καὶ πρὸς ὑμῶν ὧδʼ ἔρημος , ὦ ξένοι ,
sections.translation: And will you also , friends , leave me so desolate and show me no pity ?

sections.label: Section 1072
sections.original: ὅδʼ ἐστὶν ἡμῶν ναυκράτωρ ὁ παῖς · ὅσʼ ἂν
sections.translation: The boy commands our ship . Whatever he says to you , that is our answer also .

sections.label: Section 1074
sections.original: ἀκούσομαι μὲν ὡς ἔφυν οἴκτου πλέως
sections.translation: I shall be told by Odysseus here that I am too

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: πρὸς τοῦδʼ · ὅμως δὲ μείνατʼ , εἰ τούτῳ δοκεῖ ,
sections.translation: soft-hearted ; but remain here , if that one agrees , until the sailors have readied everything on board , and we have made our prayers to the gods . In the interval , perhaps , he will obtain a better attitude towards us . And so we two are going .

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: ὑμεῖς δʼ , ὅταν καλῶμεν , ὁρμᾶσθαι ταχεῖς .
sections.translation: And you , when we call you , be quick to come . Exeunt Odysseus and Neoptolemus .

sections.label: Section 1.1081
sections.translation: Hollow in the caverned rock , now hot , now frosty , how true it seems , then , that I was sadly fated never to leave you !

sections.label: Section 1.1090
sections.translation: Where , from what provision , shall I , unhappy , find any hope of sustenance ? Above my head the tremulous doves will go on their way through the whistling wind . I can stop their flight no more .

sections.label: Section 1.1095
sections.translation: It was you , you , I say , doomed one , that chose this fate ; and this fortune to which you are captive comes from no other source , nor from a stronger man ’ s compulsion . For when in fact it was in your power to show sense ,

sections.label: Section 1.1100
sections.original: τοῦ λῴονος δαίμονος εἵλου τὸ κάκιον αἰνεῖν .
sections.translation: you chose to reject the better fate , and to accept the worse .

sections.label: Section 1.1101
sections.translation: Ah , miserable , miserable , then , am I , and shamed by hardship , who next must hereafter dwell in my misery here ,

sections.label: Section 1.1110
sections.translation: longer gain it by the winged weapons held in my strong hands . But the unsuspected and stealthy fictions of a treacherous mind deceived me . If only I could watch him , the contriver of this plot , doomed to endure my anguish for as long a time !

sections.label: Section 1.1115
sections.translation: Doom , god-sent doom constrained you to suffer this , not , I tell you , any treachery to which my hand was lent .

sections.label: Section 1.1120
sections.translation: Aim your hate-filled , baneful curse elsewhere , since I prefer that you not reject my friendship .

sections.label: Section 2.1123
sections.translation: Alas ! No doubt sitting on the white ocean shore

sections.label: Section 2.1125
sections.translation: he mocks me , brandishing the weapon that nourished my unhappy life , the weapon which no one else had carried ! Cherished bow , ah , friend forced from my loving hands ,

sections.label: Section 2.1130
sections.original: ἦ που ἐλεινὸν ὁρᾷς , φρένας εἴ τινας
sections.translation: if you have the power to feel , surely you see with pity that the comrade of Heracles will now no longer use you anymore !

sections.label: Section 2.1135
sections.translation: Now you are handled by another , a man of craftiness ; you see the shameless deceptions and the face of that hated enemy by whom countless wrongs , springing from shameless designs , have been contrived against me . O Zeus !

sections.label: Section 2.1140
sections.original: ἀνδρός τοι τὰ μὲν ἔνδικʼ αἰὲν εἰπεῖν ,
sections.translation: A man must always assert what is right . But , when he has done so , he must not let loose malignant , stinging taunts . The man was the sole representative of the whole army , and at their mandate

sections.label: Section 2.1145
sections.translation: he achieved a universal benefit for his friends .

sections.label: Section 2.1146
sections.translation: Ah , my winged prey , and you tribes of bright-eyed creatures held by this place in its upland pastures , leap no more

sections.label: Section 2.1150
sections.translation: in flight from your lairs , for I do not grasp in my hands those shafts which were once my strength — but now , gone , are my undoing ! Roam at will now ; the place has no more terrors — for you !

sections.label: Section 2.1155
sections.translation: The time is ripe for you to take blood for blood , to sate yourselves to your heart ’ s desire on my discolored flesh ! Soon I will leave life , for from what source can I find the means to live ? Who can feed , as I will have to ,

sections.label: Section 2.1160
sections.original: πόθεν γὰρ ἔσται βιοτά ; τίς ὧδʼ ἐν αὔραις τρέφεται ,
sections.translation: on the winds , when he no longer owns any of the bounty that the life-giving earth supplies ?

sections.label: Section 2.1162
sections.original: πρὸς θεῶν , εἴ τι σέβει ξένον , πέλασσον ,
sections.translation: In the name of the gods , if you have any reverence for a friend who approaches you in all kindness , approach him !

sections.label: Section 2.1165
sections.original: ἀλλὰ γνῶθʼ , εὖ γνῶθʼ ἐπὶ σοὶ
sections.translation: But know this , and know it well : it is in your power to escape this plague . Cruel is it that you feed it with your own flesh , and that there is no way for you to learn to endure the countless torments that dwell with it .

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: πάλιν πάλιν παλαιὸν ἄλγημʼ ὑπέμνασας , ὦ
sections.translation: Again , again , you have recalled the old agony to my thoughts — kindest though you are of all who have visited before ! Why have you ruined me ? What have you done to me ?

sections.label: Section 1172
sections.original: τί μʼ ὤλεσας ; τί μʼ εἴργασαι ;
sections.translation: What do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 1173
sections.translation: That it was your hope to take

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.translation: me to that Trojan land I abhor .

sections.label: Section 1176
sections.translation: Yes , I believe that to be the best course .

sections.label: Section 1177
sections.translation: Leave me , then — immediately !

sections.label: Section 1179
sections.translation: Pleasant , pleasant , indeed , is the task you set me , and I am ready to obey .

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.translation: Come , let us be going , each to his station in the ship !

sections.label: Section 1182
sections.translation: By Zeus who hears men ’ s curses , do not go , I implore you !

sections.label: Section 1183
sections.translation: Be moderate .

sections.label: Section 1184
sections.translation: Friends , in the name of the gods , stay !

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.translation: Why do you call ?

sections.label: Section 1186
sections.translation: Ayee , ayee ! Doom , Doom ! My suffering kills me ! Foot , my foot , what shall I do with you in the days to come ?

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.translation: O friends , return , come back to me !

sections.label: Section 1191
sections.translation: To do what that has a different spirit from that of your former commands ?

sections.label: Section 1193
sections.translation: There is no reason for indignation when the words of one crazed

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.translation: by a storm of pain are senseless .

sections.label: Section 1196
sections.original: βᾶθί νυν , ὦ τάλαν , ὥς σε κελεύομεν .
sections.translation: Come with us , then , poor man , as we bid you .

sections.label: Section 1197
sections.translation: Never , never — of that be certain ! Not even if the lord of the fiery lightning comes to wrap me in the blaze of his thunderbolts !

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original: ἐρρέτω Ἴλιον οἵ θʼ ὑπʼ ἐκείνῳ
sections.translation: Ilium be damned , and as many of the men before its walls as dared reject this foot of mine ! But oh , friends , grant me one wish !

sections.label: Section 1203
sections.translation: What would you ask ?

sections.label: Section 1204
sections.original: ἢ γένυν ἢ βελέων τι προπέμψατε .
sections.translation: A sword , if you can find one , or an axe , or any weapon —

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.original: ὡς τίνα δὴ ῥέξῃς παλάμαν ποτέ ;
sections.translation: please , pass it to me !
